Day 64 - finding balance with cheat meals

Okay, maybe itâs not that dramatic ð but here are a few ways I enjoy my cheat meals guilt-free:

1. Eat a little less before the cheat meal and save those calories for it.

2. Walk. Walk. Walk.
I usually hit 10k steps every morning, but when I have a cheat meal, Iâll go for another walk afterwards. That usually puts me around 20kâ30k total steps for the day.

3. No extra restriction or excessive cardio the next day.
 I just go straight back to my normal routine.

4. Drink 3.5â4L of water the following day.
 It helps me feel better and gets rid of some of that bloated feeling from the extra sodium.

Doing this helps me stay on track while still enjoying a cheat meal once a week (although I still overthink how itâll affect my progress sometimes).

The scale usually goes up the next morning, but most of the time thatâs not body fat. Itâs usually just extra glycogen, food volume, and water retention from the higher sodium intake.

Funny enough, after a couple of days, my body often looks tighter and sometimes even better than before.

Still learning how to find balance, but this approach has helped me enjoy food without feeling like Iâm undoing all my progress.

The nostalgia hits different when you realize this movie gave us both a legendary purple modifying marvel and some of the most versatile reaction templates used on the internet today.
